name: Low-dose colchicine reduced post-MI ischemic events with a pneumonia signal
summary: COLCOT independently supports a cardiovascular outcome signal for low-dose colchicine and preserves a safety caveat.
statement: In patients enrolled within 30 days after myocardial infarction, 0.5 mg daily colchicine reduced the composite ischemic cardiovascular endpoint compared with placebo.
population_or_model: Patients recently hospitalized for myocardial infarction
quantitative_note: The primary endpoint occurred in 5.5% with colchicine versus 7.1% with placebo, HR 0.77 (95% CI 0.61 to 0.96; P=0.02); serious pneumonia was 0.9% versus 0.4%.
caveats: The outcome is post-MI secondary prevention, not healthy-lifespan extension.
Component outcomes were not uniformly reduced.
The serious pneumonia imbalance is relevant to chronic preventive anti-inflammatory use.
